_comment: CURATED public roadmap. This file is PUBLIC. Never paste internal task names, client names, teammate names, infrastructure/vendor names, or credentials here. Only ship items that are already announced in Tabatha_Changelog.md or are clearly product-facing and safe to state publicly. When in doubt, leave it out. Stages mirror the app's own FUNNEL_STAGES (src/hooks/useFocusEngine.js).

stages.label: Unsorted
stages.blurb: Captured, not yet triaged. Ideas we have written down and intend to weigh, with no commitment to build them.

stages.label: Todo
stages.blurb: Accepted and queued. The shape of the work is understood; it is waiting on a slot, not on a decision.

stages.label: Focus
stages.blurb: The next thing up. Designed and scheduled, starting imminently.

stages.label: Addressing
stages.blurb: Actively being built right now. Partially landed in the codebase, not yet in a release you can install.

stages.label: Roadblocked
stages.blurb: Blocked on something else finishing first. Real work, parked on purpose rather than abandoned.

stages.label: Resolved
stages.blurb: Shipped and installable. Each card names the version it landed in.

items.title: Unified Calendar
items.blurb: A real month, week, and day calendar inside Tabatha, with your focuses and stints on it, plus a sidebar agenda for the day ahead. The data layer is built; the calendar UI and the account connection are what remain.

items.title: URL Rules Intelligence
items.blurb: Tabatha learns which sites belong to which context instead of making you write every rule by hand. The first pass of the rules engine shipped; the training mode that suggests rules from your own history is in progress.

items.title: Activity timeline editing
items.blurb: Correct a tracked day directly on the timeline: trim a range, split a block, mark a break that the tracker recorded as work.

items.title: Intent to Focus pipeline
items.blurb: Promote an intent you declared at the Gatekeeper into a tracked focus without retyping it, so the thing you said you were doing becomes the thing being measured.

items.title: Asana integration
items.blurb: Link an Asana task to a focus so time tracked in the browser lands against the task it belongs to. Detection and linking work; the setup guide and a status indicator in Settings are outstanding.

items.title: Chrome Web Store listing
items.blurb: Tabatha currently installs and self-updates through its own signed channel. Publishing to the Web Store is the remaining distribution step.

items.title: Smart deferral and task splitting
items.blurb: Defer a task to a future stint and let Tabatha find and size the slot from your real schedule, instead of pushing a date and hoping.

items.title: Backburner
items.blurb: Park a focus you are blocked on, switch to something else honestly, and get reminded to come back to it rather than quietly dropping it.

items.title: Calendar auto-backfill
items.blurb: Turn calendar events you actually attended into time entries after the fact, so meeting-heavy days do not have to be reconstructed from memory.

items.title: Meeting block detection
items.blurb: Recognise when you are in a meeting and switch tracking into meeting time automatically, instead of logging it as browsing.

items.title: Priority matrix and lazy priority
items.blurb: Two tiers of priority: a fast P1 to P5 pass for everyday sorting, and an urgency-versus-relevance matrix for when the fast pass is not enough.

items.title: Voice input
items.blurb: Speak an intent or a note instead of typing it, on the fields where typing is the thing that stops you from logging at all.

items.title: InPop intent switcher
items.blurb: Make the in-page popup header a full intent switcher, so changing what a tab is for does not require opening another surface.

items.title: Priority challenge interrupts
items.blurb: Varied, fatigue-resistant prompts that ask whether this is genuinely the most important thing right now. Under consideration: interrupts that get ignored are worse than no interrupts.

items.title: Focus and task model cleanup
items.blurb: Normalise the distinction between a focus and a task, and make promoting one into the other a first-class path rather than a convention.

items.title: Org-wide analytics
items.blurb: Cross-member analytics for org admins, building on the per-person analytics that already ship.

items.title: Parallel focuses
items.blurb: Hold more than one focus at once for genuinely concurrent work. Deliberately blocked on the focus and task model cleanup landing first, because building it on the current model would be rework.

items.title: Multi-track timeline editing
items.blurb: A deeper editor over the activity timeline with multiple tracks and contribution notes. Waiting on the single-track timeline editing work to finish first.

items.title: Survives a restart
items.blurb: The extension no longer disappears after a computer restart. It installs to a stable, self-healing location instead of a build folder.

items.title: Backdating moves the start
items.blurb: Setting an earlier start time always takes effect. Overlapping work is reported back to you instead of being silently refused.

items.title: Honest time-edit limits
items.blurb: When an edit is capped by your clock-in or by the current time, the editor says so and offers the earliest time it can accept.

items.title: Self-hosted update channel
items.blurb: Installs update themselves in the background, on login and every six hours, with a verified download and no store review in the loop.

items.title: Component showcase
items.blurb: Every major surface and component rendered from the real design system. The page you are reading is the site it grew into.

items.title: Work schedule profiles
items.blurb: Per-person profiles for dedicated hours or self-managed time, with required-hours floors, a weekly schedule editor, and a change-request flow.

items.title: Shortfall tracking
items.blurb: Under-hours periods are recorded at clock-out and can be made up, shifted, or excused, rather than vanishing.

items.title: Schedule view
items.blurb: Three modes in Work Shifts: your own schedule, team roster management, and an approval inbox for incoming requests.

items.title: Work Shifts analytics
items.blurb: Daily hours, weekly comparison, peak productivity hours, break patterns, time per focus, context switching, and CSV export. Every chart is hand-rolled CSS; the extension ships no charting library.

items.title: Time-editing overhaul
items.blurb: Type a duration like 500m or 8h20m to set, add, or subtract time, with a live preview and Enter to apply.

items.title: Offline-gap detector
items.blurb: If your machine was asleep or off while tracking, Tabatha spots the gap on wake and asks you to confirm, credit, or adjust it. No phantom hours.

items.title: Abandoned stint recovery
items.blurb: Stints orphaned by a previous session surface when you next clock in, with an end-time picker to correct them.

items.title: Settings search
items.blurb: Press / in Settings to fuzzy-find any option across all 22 sections. Status chips click through to the panel that owns them.

items.title: Storage quota fix
items.blurb: Fixed a quota problem where pause, resume, and other changes appeared to work but were never written to disk.

items.title: Cloud Sync reliability
items.blurb: Account and display-name changes are queued durably and retried instead of hanging, and signing in now syncs automatically.

items.title: Live Stints accuracy
items.blurb: Live Stints no longer shows ghost cards for installs that are gone, and no longer silently does nothing right after startup.
